item.title: baby todo or not todo
item.description: INTRODUCTION This challenge is a docker container, coming packaged with a zip of all of the whole docker container (it’s a “white box” challenge.) Open up the source code and read through it, get a sense of how the app is supposed to work. The name of the folder indicates “broken authentication control”, so that’s probably where this is headed. FIRST TAKE The db file schema.sql is the first file to read.

item.title: baby WAFfles order
item.description: FIRST TAKE Download the challenge files: it is a docker, showing you the source code for the whole challenge. Obvious hint: the title of the website is xxe Read all the source code, understand how the web app works. In essence, the challenge is an order-taking API for a fictional restaurant, taking orders for either Ice Scream or WAFfles. Orders may be submitted to /api/order as: application/json application/xml CONVERTING REQUEST TO XML It appears that the website is configured to submit orders as json, but the endpoint will still accept xml.

item.title: Phonebook
item.description: INTRODUCTION At the time of writing this walkthrough, Phonebook is currently active (Oct 2021) and worth 30 points. FIRST TAKE I started up the challenge and visited the website. It is a small login form. Looks pretty typical. Checking the source code for another look reveals a conspicuous little script: <script> const queryString = window.location.search; if (queryString) { const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(queryString); const message = urlParams.get('message'); if (message) { document.
